results.title: The calculation of totalAssets() could be wrong if operatorFeeAmount > 0, this can cause potential loss for the new depositors
results.reasoning: Yes. Audit item #8 (“totalAssets Inflation by Unreduced operatorFeeAmount Enables Share Price Arbitrage”) describes the same bug: operatorFeeAmount is not subtracted in LiquidRon.totalAssets(), inflating share price and causing new depositors to overpay and later withdraw less when the fee is fetched. It points to the same code path (LiquidRon.totalAssets) and recommends the same fix (subtract operatorFeeAmount). Fixing the audit’s issue would fix the described vulnerability.
